goal: Reconcile usability fix: drop actor limit, add tree-hash and commit-hash matching, expose 'mainline reconcile <intent> <commit>' for manual link; CommitNote gains via=reconcile_auto/manual and match_strategy. Spec → v0.1-rc4 patch.

summary.title: Reconcile usability fix: tree-hash matching, drop actor limit, manual pin command
summary.what: Rewrote Service.Reconcile to walk a strategy cascade (tree_hash → commit_hash → goal_text), removed the iv.ActorID==identity.ActorID restriction so any teammate can attach a reconcile note to any proposed intent, added Service.ReconcileManual for explicit (intent, commit) pinning, added Git.CommitTreeHash, extended CommitNote with via=reconcile_auto/reconcile_manual and a match_strategy field, added sync.normaliseVia to keep view-layer merged_via stable across legacy and new note formats. CLI 'mainline reconcile [intent commit]' now supports both auto (no args) and manual (two args). Wrote spec patch docs_for_ai/mainline-spec-v0.1-rc4-patch.md and reconcile_test.go covering tree_hash, commit_hash, cross-actor, manual happy path, manual guards, and via backward-compat.
summary.why: Self-dogfooding v0.1-rc3 surfaced a P0 design seam: GitHub web UI merge (the default path most teams use) leaves a commit on main with subject 'Merge pull request #N from ...' that does not contain the intent goal, so reconcile's only heuristic — strings.Contains(msg, goal) — has 0% hit rate. Combined with the actor-id filter that skipped foreign intents, reconcile could not close the loop on any GitHub-merged work, requiring a long 'git notes --ref=mainline/intents add' incantation by hand. Squash merge preserves the commit tree byte-for-byte, so a tree-hash compare is the right primary strategy. Lifting the actor restriction is correct because notes live on shared main commits, not in anyone's private actor log.
summary.user_goal: Make 'mainline reconcile' actually work for the GitHub PR merge path so the project stops requiring manual git-notes incantations.

decisions.point: Where to compute the intent tree hash for matching
decisions.rationale: Avoids a CommitNote/IntentSealedEvent schema change; every existing sealed intent benefits without migration. A few extra git rev-parse calls per reconcile is cheap.
rejected: Persist intent.code_tree at seal time — needs schema bump and a migration for already-sealed intents.

decisions.rationale: Notes are written to shared main commits under a separate ref, not into anyone's actor log. The restriction was protecting a non-existent invariant. Note.added_by still records who reconciled.
rejected: Keep restriction but allow override via flag — adds friction for the common case.

decisions.rationale: tree_hash is the strongest signal under squash (the dominant default in GitHub). commit_hash catches fast-forward and no-ff. goal_text remains as a weak fallback for mainline-merge-written commits whose subject is the intent goal.

decisions.chose: Add normaliseVia in sync; reader collapses reconcile/reconcile_auto/reconcile_manual/manual all to 'reconcile' for view consumers
decisions.rationale: Notes already on main from rc3 and the manual git-notes-add I did earlier still resolve correctly; downstream code that switches on iv.StatusEvidence.MergedVia keeps the existing two-value contract.
rejected: One-shot rewrite of all existing notes — would require force-pushing the notes ref.

decisions.rationale: Auto-writing shared refs from a passive command (sync) is a more sensitive default; want one rc cycle of explicit reconcile in the wild before flipping it.

risks: tree_hash false positives are theoretically possible if two unrelated intents produce identical trees (e.g. both empty diffs). Mitigation: alreadyHasIntent check prevents double-stamping the same intent on the same commit; cross-intent tree collisions still possible but extremely unlikely in practice — would land the wrong intent on the wrong commit.

ReconcileManual takes a commit hash from the operator without confirming it lives on main; that's deliberate so cherry-picked / non-mainline cases work, but it lets a typo land an intent on an unintended commit. Mitigated only by the proposed-status guard.
followups: v0.2: auto-reconcile after sync for tree_hash + commit_hash (config opt-out).
v0.5: optional GitHub Action that writes the reconcile note on PR merge automatically.
Consider 'mainline link' as a CLI alias: reconcile semantically implies 'fix divergence' but in the rc4 model it is the normal post-PR step.
PBT for tree_hash false positives — fuzz two intents with overlapping diffs and check the alreadyHasIntent guard never silently overwrites.
Add an opt-in confirmation prompt for ReconcileManual when the commit message obviously does not relate (best-effort heuristic for safety).

architectural_claims: Reconcile is the canonical post-PR step on the GitHub web UI merge path; it must succeed automatically on the squash-merge default.
Notes are shared main-commit metadata, not actor-private state; therefore any actor may write a reconcile note for any intent.
via and match_strategy are write-time facts about how a note came to exist; merged_via in the view is a stable two-value summary derived from them.
behavioral_changes: mainline reconcile now matches GitHub squash-merge commits via tree_hash and writes via=reconcile_auto.
mainline reconcile <intent> <commit> is a new manual pin command writing via=reconcile_manual.
mainline reconcile no longer skips intents owned by other actors.
View consumers of merged_via see only 'merge' or 'reconcile' regardless of which sub-via the note used.

api_changes.signature: (*engine.Service).Reconcile() (*ReconcileResult, error) — Result now carries Links []ReconciledLink with match_strategy

data_model_changes.name: CommitNote
data_model_changes.migration_notes: via gains reconcile_auto/reconcile_manual values; legacy reconcile and manual still parse via normaliseVia. New optional match_strategy field; absence is fine.

security_implications: Lifting the actor restriction means any teammate can claim any intent as merged via reconcile. Mitigated by the alreadyHasIntent dedup guard and by the audit trail in note.added_by; no integrity loss because notes are not signed in v0.1 anyway.
migration_notes: Notes written by rc3 (via=reconcile) and notes I hand-wrote earlier (via=manual) keep working. New writers should emit reconcile_auto / reconcile_manual.
